hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Turn — Ace. You checked. Your opponent bet — bigger than expected.

wrongReasons: 9 5 has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with 9 5.
4 2 makes a straight here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
10 2 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only high card wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 5 A

Your opponent called the flop with one pair, then picked up two pair on the turn. The sudden aggression on the turn — after passivity on the flop — is the tell: they improved and immediately started building the pot.
